content: Task: Analyze arbitrage-trader codebase state and identify meaningful work that can be done while waiting for Kalshi API access. All 8 Rust crates are built and compiling.
Delegated to Planning Lead: analyze arbitrage-trader codebase state, identify all work doable without Kalshi credentials
Delegated to Architect: read all 8 crates, check tests, find TODOs, compare SPEC vs implementation, produce prioritized action plan
Full codebase analysis complete. 167 tests passing. Zero TODOs/FIXMEs. Found 1 bug (--all-features compilation), 1 risk (Kalshi API URL mismatch), 12 prioritized actions across 4 priority tiers.
Reviewed Architect output. Quality confirmed. Synthesized prioritized action plan with 4 priority tiers, 12 actions. Key finding: codebase is impressively complete, not stubbed.
Planning team analysis complete. Presenting synthesized results to user.
User approved quick wins. Delegated to Engineering Lead: execute 4 quick fixes (--all-features bug, Kalshi URL, pairs.toml, .env.example)
Delegated all 4 fixes to Backend Dev in single prompt. Then delegated Code Reviewer for review.

All 4 quick wins implemented. Fix 1: added poly_yes_token_id to 3 test structs in engine.rs. Fix 2: updated Kalshi URLs in default.toml. Fix 3: added 4 example pairs to pairs.toml. Fix 4: created comprehensive .env.example. cargo test --all-features passes 170 tests.

All changes reviewed. APPROVE. poly_yes_token_id values match mock order book keys. URL fix matches KalshiConfig defaults. pairs.toml matches TomlPair struct. .env.example is comprehensive. 170 tests passing, no regressions.
All 4 quick wins done. Code review APPROVED. 170 tests passing (up from 167 — the 3 previously-failing mock tests now compile and pass).
